channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Death of ex-One Direction member Liam Payne at 31 shocks fans around the world
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The death of Liam Payne, who shot to stardom as a member of the boy band One Direction and grappled with intense global fame while still in his teens, sent shockwaves across the world Thursday as Argentine authorities continued their investigation at the scene.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's former armed forces chief endorses 'victory plan' in first speech since his dismissal
item.description: Ukraine’s former commander-in-chief, Valerii Zaluzhnyi, has expressed his support for President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s “victory plan,” despite the tensions between the two that led to his dismissal in February.
item.dc:creator: Susie Blann And Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How did as many as 140 people die in one of Nigeria's deadliest tanker explosions?
item.description: Nigerian authorities have announced measures to correct lapses that resulted in the death of more than 140 people in an explosion as they were trying to scoop up fuel from an overturned fuel tanker on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Salim Umar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean court acquits former police chief over deadly crowd crush
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court found the former police chief of the country’s capital and two other officers not guilty over a botched response to a Halloween crowd crush that killed nearly 160 people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US hits Houthis with precision strikes on weapon facilities in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. announced late Wednesday that it had conducted multiple strikes on Yemen’s Houthi rebel group, targeting its underground bunkers with stealth bombers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military unleashed B-2 stealth bombers to target underground bunkers used by Yemen’s Houthi rebels early Thursday, a major escalation in the American response to the rebels' attacks on Mideast shipping lanes that appeared to be a warning to Iran as well.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Moldova becomes ‘testing ground’ for Russian election interference
item.description: Russian efforts to influence Moldova’s Oct. 20 elections have reached unprecedented levels, with Moscow allegedly spending up to hundreds of millions of dollars on buying votes, pumping out AI-generated deep fake videos, and boosting the campaigns of Kremlin-favored candidates.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Biden travels to Berlin for summit with European leaders
item.description: CORRECTION: President Biden is traveling to Germany on Thursday. An earlier version of this story misstated the timing of his arrival. President Biden is traveling to Germany on Thursday after postponing travel for a week because of Hurricane Milton striking the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Bangladesh court issues arrest warrant for ex-Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina over deaths of protesters
item.description: A special court in Bangladesh has issued arrest warrants for former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina and 45 others, including her close aides, on charges of crimes against humanity during a student-led uprising in July and August that forced her to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zelenskyy says 10,000 North Koreans could join Russian forces in Ukraine as he pushes 'Victory Plan'
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says his country has intelligence information that 10,000 troops from North Korea are being prepared to join Russian forces fighting against his country, warning that a third nation wading into the hostilities would turn the conflict into a “world war.”.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder And Raf Casert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Aging farmers face extreme temperatures as they struggle to maintain Japan's rice crop
item.description: KAMIMOMI, Japan (AP) — In the remote village of Kamimomi in Japan’s western Okayama prefecture, a small group of rice farmers began their most recent harvest in sweltering heat, two weeks sooner than usual.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA MCGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zeta drug lord continued to control cartel from inside a Mexican prison, says US indictment
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A U.S. indictment unsealed Wednesday in the District of Columbia claims that the leader of one of Mexico’s most violent gangs continued to run an offshoot group, the Northeast Cartel, from inside a Mexican prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau says Canadian police went public with Indian diplomat allegations to prevent more violence
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Wednesday that Canada's national police force went public with its allegations against Indian diplomats this week because it had to disrupt violent acts in Canada including drive-by-shootings, extortions and even murder.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Palestinian student was asleep in his tent at a hospital when an Israeli strike brought an inferno
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Shaban al-Dalu was sleeping in his tent in a central Gaza hospital's courtyard, still recuperating from wounds from an Israeli strike on a mosque a week earlier, when a new strike hit, setting off an inferno.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces $425 million security aid package for Ukraine
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday discussed U.S. support for Ukraine in its defense against Russian aggression during a call with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and announced a new $425 million military aid package.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside Mexico's Sinaloa drug cartel. Cartel gunmen stop youths on the street or in their cars and demand their phones.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen Cotton says Biden-Harris likely prolonged Gaza war, let aid go to terrorists: 'Betrayed' taxpayers
item.description: Amid Israel’s existential seven-front war to root out terrorists on its borders, Arkansas GOP Sen. Tom Cotton says U.S. humanitarian aid might have got into the hands of terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woodward compares Putin to Hitler, says Russian leader 'trying to play' Trump
item.description: Veteran journalist and author Bob Woodward compared Russian President Vladimir Putin to Nazi leader Adolf Hitler, claiming Putin is “trying to play” former President Trump, putting the U.S. in dangerous spot if he returns to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Leaders from China-Russia-backed international organization meet in Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Leaders and top officials from an international group founded to counter Western alliances met in Pakistan's capital on Wednesday and called for enhanced cooperation in areas including security, trade, and health.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEE IT: Suspect discovered hiding in couch on police bodycam while serving arrest warrant
item.description: Bodycam footage from police in the UK show a game of hide-and-seek that ended poorly for one suspect being served an arrest warrant over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says Yemen risks being dragged into Mideast conflict that could spiral out of control
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Yemen risks being dragged further into the military escalation in the Middle East that keeps intensifying and could spiral out of control, the U.N. special envoy for the Arab world’s poorest nation said Tuesday. Hans Grundberg told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez fires back at Fetterman’s 'bleak dunk attempt' on Gaza
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) fired back at Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) over his “bleak dunk attempt” on the ongoing war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Mexican president acknowledges army killings of 3 civilians in a violent border city
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president acknowledged Tuesday that three civilians including a child died in two shootouts involving the military last week in a violent border city.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel reveals Hezbollah special forces terrorist 'bunker' located under home with weapons, motorcycles
item.description: Israel's military has uncovered a bunker it says Hezbollah's special forces unit was using to plan a cross-border attack on Israeli citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father called UK police to confess to killing daughter, 10, in England after he fled to Pakistan
item.description: The father of a 10-year-old girl who was found dead in England called U.K. police, after flying to Pakistan, to say that he had killed his daughter, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces seize documents that reveal Hamas plan for more elaborate attacks: report
item.description: Hundreds of documents seized by Israeli forces reveal a broader ambition by Hamas to plot attacks against Israel in the years before Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China surrounds Taiwan in military exercise against independence
item.description: China has launched massive military exercises surrounding Taiwan in response to the island refusing to follow the rule of the Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
